installerAreasChecked: src/utils/velt-docs-fetcher.js — FEATURE_SKILL_MAP (4 skill names checked against agent-skills/skills/)
src/utils/plan-formatter.js — setup step sequences, component/import references
src/utils/validation.js — VeltProvider placement check, VeltCommentsSidebar placement check, @veltdev/react dependency check

docEvidence.text:
---
title: Overview
description: CRDT (Yjs) based collaborative editing.
---

drifts.description: VELT_DOCS_URLS.crdt.overview points to 'https://docs.velt.dev/multiplayer-editing/overview', but the directory 'multiplayer-editing/' does not exist in the docs repo. The actual CRDT overview lives at 'realtime-collaboration/crdt/overview.mdx', which maps to 'https://docs.velt.dev/realtime-collaboration/crdt/overview'. Any fetch of this URL (e.g., via getDocMarkdownUrl for the crdt overview) will 404.
drifts.suggestedFix:
In src/utils/velt-docs-urls.js line 64, change:
  overview: 'https://docs.velt.dev/multiplayer-editing/overview',
to:
  overview: 'https://docs.velt.dev/realtime-collaboration/crdt/overview',

installerEvidence.text:
setup: {
  freestyle: 'https://docs.velt.dev/async-collaboration/comments/setup/freestyle',
  popover: '...',
  page: '...',
  text: '...',
  inline: '...',
  tiptap: '...',
  lexical: '...',
  slate: '...'
  // stream is absent
}

drifts.description: The 'stream' comment mode has a docs setup page at async-collaboration/comments/setup/stream.mdx and a test instruction in plan-formatter.js (line 105), but has no entry in VELT_DOCS_URLS.comments.setup. When 'stream' is passed as commentType to getDocUrl('comments', 'stream'), the lookup falls through to the featureConfig.setup fallback (line 110 of velt-docs-urls.js), returning the base setup object rather than the stream-specific URL. Any markdown fetch for stream comments will silently use the wrong URL.
drifts.suggestedFix:
In src/utils/velt-docs-urls.js, add 'stream' to VELT_DOCS_URLS.comments.setup after line 27:
  slate: 'https://docs.velt.dev/async-collaboration/comments/setup/slatejs',
  stream: 'https://docs.velt.dev/async-collaboration/comments/setup/stream',

// Check 3: VeltProvider in layout
...
const layoutPaths = [
  'app/layout.tsx',
  'app/layout.js',
  'src/app/layout.tsx',
  'src/app/layout.js',
];
...
hasVeltProvider = layoutContent.includes('VeltProvider');
...
name: 'VeltProvider configured',
status: hasVeltProvider ? 'pass' : 'fail',
message: hasVeltProvider ? 'VeltProvider found in layout' : 'VeltProvider not found in layout'

docEvidence.text:
The installer recommends placing `VeltProvider` in `app/page.tsx`, not `app/layout.tsx`. Follow this recommendation unless you have a specific reason to do otherwise.
...
├── page.tsx                # Contains VeltProvider with authProvider hook

drifts.description: validation.js Check 3 (validateInstallation) searches only layout.tsx files for VeltProvider and marks it 'fail' if not found there. However, mcp-installer.mdx explicitly states VeltProvider belongs in app/page.tsx, not app/layout.tsx. plan-formatter.js (lines 158, 292) also enforces 'VeltProvider goes in ${locationText}, NOT in layout.tsx'. A correctly installed project will always fail this check, producing false 'VeltProvider not found in layout' errors and an incorrect score.

docEvidence.text:
└── VeltCollaboration.tsx       # All Velt feature components
...
├── page.tsx                # Contains VeltProvider with authProvider hook

drifts.description: validation.js Check 5 looks for 'VeltCommentsSidebar' in layout.tsx. In the installer's own scaffolding pattern, all Velt feature components (including VeltCommentsSidebar) are encapsulated inside the CLI-generated VeltCollaboration.tsx, which is placed inside VeltProvider in page.tsx — not in layout.tsx. A correctly installed project will always fail this check. Additionally, the check only activates when layoutFound=true (from Check 3's layout file scan), creating a dependency on the already-broken Check 3 logic.
